description: The Prussian commanded by General Pirch on June 15th 1815, retreat had scarcely begun when his retreating columns were vigorously assaulted by four Squadrons de Service of the Imperial Guard commanded by General Letort.

name: General von Pirch tomb
description: He began his career in the Prussian army in 1775. At Waterloo Pirch handed over the command of II Corps to Prince Augustus of Prussia.

name: Gerhard von Scharnhorst tomb
description: He was a Prussian general and the most important military theorist and reformer of the Prussian army in the Napoleonic Wars.

name: Statue General Gerhard von Scharnhorst
description: He was a Prussian general and the most important military theorist and reformer of the Prussian army in the Napoleonic Wars.

name: Bust General Gerhard von Scharnhorst
description: He was a Prussian general and the most important military theorist and reformer of the Prussian army in the Napoleonic Wars.

name: Hesse Haus
description: House were it is said Genaral von Blücher stayed for on January 10th 1814.

name: Ottweiler old castle square
description: Place where the scene portrayed by Christian Sell for his 'Prince Blücher's address to the population, January 1814' in a series of historical postcards for the District Veterans' Association (Bezirkskriegerverband) in the early 20th century.

name: Battlefield of Heliópolis
description: French forces under General Kléber defeated the Ottoman army led by Nassif Pasha on March 20th 1800.

name: Battlefield of Alexandria
description: Also known as the Battle of Canope/Canopus. It was fought on March 21st 1801, where the British army and naval forces repelled an assault by the French army.

name: Siege of El Arish
description: The Siege of El Arish was a siege of the Ottoman fortress of El Arish by the French army, which took place from February 8th to 20th 1801 during the French campaign in Egypt.

name: Battlefield of Mandora
description: It was a minor battle fought on March 13th 1801 between French forces under François Lanusse and the British expeditionary corps under Ralph Abercromby.

name: Napoleon's monument
description: A pyramid-shaped memorial located directly in front of the Stella Maris Monastery on Mount Carmel. It commemorates French soldiers from Napoleon Bonaparte's army sick of the plague in 1799 and were burried there.

name: Siege of Acre
description: It was an unsuccessful French two month siege of the Ottoman city started on March 20th 1799 and was the turning point of Napoleon's invasion of Egypt and Syria

name: Battlefield of Lübeck
description: It took place on November 6th 1806 between soldiers of of Prussia led by Blücher, who were retreating from defeat at the Battle of Jena-Auerstedt, and French troops under Marshals Murat, Bernadotte, and Soult

name: Prise de Ratisbonne
description: Canvas by Johann Lorenz Rugendas the Younger. It depicts the storming of Ratisbon by the French army on April 23rd 1809 during the War of the Fifth Coalition.

name: Capture of Alexandria
description: On July 2nd 1798, the French army landed and took the city of Alexandria from the hands of the Janissaries.

name: Battlefield of Chebreiss
description: Also known as Shubra Khit,was the second major engagement of the French invasion of Egypt and Syria and took place on July 13th 1798.

name: Battlefield of Sediman
description: Also known as Battle of El Lahun, took place on October 7th 1798. French troops under General Louis Desaix defeated the Mamluk forces led by Murad Bey, securing control over Upper Egypt.

name: Battlefield of Jaffa
description: The French laid siege to the city of Jaffa, which was under Ottoman control. It was fought from March 3rd to the 7th 1799, when French forces managed to capture the city.

name: Battlefield of Mohrungen
description: On January 25th 1807, most of a First French Empire corps under the leadership of Marshal Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte fought a strong Russian Empire advance guard led by Major General Yevgeni Ivanovich Markov.

name: Battlefield of Allenstein
description: Also known as the Battle of Jonkowo and the Battle of Bergfriede. The battle on February 3rd 1807 resulted in a French field victory and allowed for a successful pursuit of the Russian army.

name: Bergfriede bridge - Allenstein
description: Towards the end of the afternoon, Soult, with the 24th Light and 4th Line regiments began his flanking attack and, after some ferocious combat, pushed the Russians beyond the Alle, capturing the Bergfriede bridge.

name: Napoleon's observation point - Allenstein
description: From the church tower, Napoleon observed the disposition of the main Russian army, spread between Jonkowo, Redykajny, and Gutkowo, which influenced his decision to avoid a general engagement (he had previously thought it was only a small detachment).

name: Napoleon's memorial - Allenstein
description: Napoleon's Memorial Stone in Olsztyn, next to Łyna (Alle) river, with a bilingual Polish and French inscription.

name: Olsztyn Marquet Square - Allenstein
description: At this square Napoleon received battle reports and commanded the battle of Allenstein.

name: Russian left wing- Allenstein
description: The left wing of the Russian army ended around this position.

name: Three Crosses Square in Olsztyn- Allenstein
description: Place of burial of French and Russian soldiers who died at the battle of Allenstein

name: Battlefield of Dürenstein (Krems)
description: Also known as he Battle of Krems. On November 11th 1805 a combined force of Russian and Austrian troops trapped a French division commanded by Théodore Maxime Gazan.

name: 'Little Frenchman' memorial - Dürenstein
description: French memorial to the soldiers who died at the battle of Dürenstein.

name: Battlefield of Sabugal
description: It was a meeting engagement of the Peninsular War which took place on April 3rd 1811 between Anglo-Portuguese forces under Arthur Wellesley (later the Duke of Wellington) and French troops under the command of Marshal André Masséna.

name: Wellington's Command Post - Bussaco
description: Wellington's command post during the battle.

name: Crauford's Command Post - Bussaco
description: General's Crauford command post during the battle.

name: Battlefield of Bussaco
description: It was fought on September 27th 1810 during the Peninsular War in the Portuguese mountain range of Serra do Buçaco, resulting in the defeat of French forces by Lord Wellington's Anglo-Portuguese Army.

name: Monument Battle of Bussaco
description: Constructed in 1873, the obelisk was greatly damaged by lightning in 1876 and was restored in 1878-1879.

name: Massena's Command Post - Bussaco
description: Remains of the windmill which served as Masséna's command post at Moura.

name: English Cemetery at Elvas
description: It is one of the oldest British Military Cemeteries in existence. It holds only five known graves but two of these for the tallen at Albuera and another for the fallen at Badajoz.

name: Lines of Torres Vedras
description: Lines of Torres Vedras were lines of forts and other military defences built in secrecy to defend Lisbon during the Peninsular War.

name: Duke of Wellington's Headquarters at Pero Negro
description: During the construction of the fortificaions of Lines of Arthur Wellesley established his headquarters at this residence.

name: Puente de Barba del Puerco
location: San Felices de los Gallegos, Salamanca
description: Uno de los cuatro cruces del río Águeda, fue volado en abril de 1811 por un ingeniero Portugués para cortar el paso a las tropas del General Marmont.

name: Wellington's Headquarters in Freineda
description: During the winters of 1811-1812 and 1812-1813, Wellington set his headquarters in a small Portuguese village about 25 kilometers from the Spanish border fortress of Ciudad Rodrigo.

name: Núcleo Interpretativo da Batalha da Roliça
description: Inaugurado a 17 de novembro de 2024 a memória do histórico confronto militar, ocorrido a 17 de agosto de 1808

name: French invasion of Malta
description: The French First Republic led by Napoleon Bonaparte successfully invaded the islands of Malta and Gozo, then ruled by the Knights Hospitaller, in June 10th 1798 as part of the Meiterranean 1798 of the French Revolutionary Wars.

name: General Vaubois landing point - Malta
description: On the morning of June 10th, the French began landing, General Vaubois landed between St. Julien and La Madelaine

name: General Desaix landing point - Malta
description: On the morning of June 10th, the French began landing, General Desaix landed at the eastern cost of the island.

name: General Bonaparte landing point - Malta
description: On the morning of June 10th, the French began landing, General Bonaparte landed nort-west of La Valeta.

name: French Memorial Fallen in Egypt Campaing
description: A memorial stone commemorating the French soldiers and participants of Napoleon's Egyptian Expedition located in the Latin Cemetery in Cairo.

name: Battle of Cape Santa María
description: A British squadron under the command of Commodore Graham Moore attacked and defeated a Spanish squadron led by Brigadier Don José de Bustamante.

name: Battle of Sandöström
description: The Swedes, whose fleet controlled the open sea, attempted to remove the Russian reinforcements.

name: Battle of Palva Sound
description: The Battle of Palva Sund was fought between Sweden and Russia on September 18th 1808.

name: Battlefield of Gamonal
description: On November 11th 1808, a French army under Soult overwhelmed the outnumbered Spanish troops under General Belvedere, opening central Spain, including Madrid, to invasion.

name: General Jirčik Memorial - Austerlitz
description: This memorial commemorates the soldiers of Jirčík's Brigade who were killed in the Battle of Austerlitz.

name: Bohdalice Palace
description: On November 29th 1805, Russian Tzar Alexander and Francis I Emperor of Austria spent the night at this venue, shortly before the Battle of Austerlitz.

name: Brno Obelisk
description: It was erected in 1818 to commemorate the end of long-lasting war miseries.

name: Šlapanice church
description: It was erected in 1818 to commemorate the end of long-lasting war miseries.

name: Zuran Hill - Austerlitz
description: Napoleon's command post during the battle of Austerlitz.

name: Santon Hill - Austerlitz
description: Here laid the left flank of the French army. The 17th Light Infantry Regiment, under the command of General Claparéde, was chosen for its defence.

name: Murat's HQ - Austerlitz
description: Before the Battle of Austerlitz, the place was utilized by French Marshal Murat as his headquartes. Later it was in hands of Prince Bagration who started his attack on the Santon Hill from here.

name: Action of 25 September 1806
description: A French squadron comprising five frigates and two corvettes, sailing to the French West Indies with supplies and reinforcements, was intercepted by a British squadron of six ships of the line.

name: 'Jeune Voltigeur' - Toulouse battle
description: The work of artist Sonia Queija was inaugurated on February 15, 2025. The monument pays tribute to the soldiers who participated in the Battle of Toulouse in 1814.

name: Copenhagen (Wellington's horse) grave site
description: Copenhagen died on 12 February 1836 at Stratfield Saye, Duke of Wellington property at Hampshire in England, at the age of 28 years, and is burried under a large Turkey Oak tree.

name: Museo Storico Nazionale dell'Artiglieria
description: The Museo Storico Nazionale dell'Artiglieria is a museum in Turin, Italy, that houses a collection of military artillery from the Napoleonic era.

name: Friedland field hospital
description: During the Battle of Friedland, this building housed a hospital where wounded Russian soldiers were taken, and it is perhaps this building that L. N. Tolstoy describes in his novel War and Peace.

name: Friedland
location: Metropolitan Museum of Arts, New York
description: Friedland is a painting by the French artist Ernest Meissonier, depicting the Battle of Friedland in 1807. It is a gift of Henry Hilton to the Metropolitan Museum of Arts in 1887.

name: Napoleon in His Study at the Tuileries
description: Napoleon in His Study at the Tuileries is a painting by the French artist Jacques-Louis David, depicting Napoleon Bonaparte in his study at the Tuileries Palace in 1800. It is in the National Gallery of Art, West Building Main Floor, Gallery 56, in Washington D.C., USA.

name: Louis Bonaparte's tomb
description: Louis Bonaparte's tomb is in the Church of Saint-Leu-Saint-Gilles, Saint-Leu-la-Forêt, France. It is a sarcophagus in the choir of the church, and it is a gift of Napoleon Bonaparte to his brother Louis.

name: Kutuzov's bust at Friedland
description: Although Kutuzov did not took part in the Campaign in Poland there is a bust of him in the Russian town of Friedland, which is today Pravdinsk.

name: Vive l'Empereur!- Friedland
location: Art Gallery of New South Wales, Sydney
description: Jean Baptiste Édouard Detaille's magnificent Charge of the 4th Hussars at the battle of Friedland was purchased in 1893 by the Art Gallery of New South Wales and is exhibited at the Naala Nura, ground level, Grand Courts.

name: Russian soldiers memorial - Friedland
description: Dedicated to the Russian soldiers who fought in the Battle of Friedland in 1807, it was erected in 1992.

name: Napoleon's HQ after the battle of Friedland
description: Napoleon used this building as his HQ after the battle of Friedland in 1807.

name: Battle of Friedland
description: This artwork by Horace Vernet, represents Napoleon at the Battle of Friedland giving instructions to general Nicolas Oudinot. It is exhibited in the Hall of Battles in the Palace of Versailles.

name: Napoleon in Egypt
location: Princeton University Art Museum, Princeton, New Jersey
description: This artwork by Jean-Léon Gérôme, also called Napoleon in front of the Sphinx, represents Napoleon Bonaparte in Egypt.

name: Battlefield of Lodi
description: Fought on May 10th 1796, Bonaparte won a decisive victory against the Austrians.

name: Austrian position at Corte Palasio - Lodi
description: Sebotendorf located 2,100 infantes and 300 Austrian troopers near the villa of 'Corte Palasio' to protect a ford in the Adda river

name: Lodi - First action 9am
description: The French advance guard caught up with Josef Vukassovich's Austrian rear-guard at about 9 am on 10 May and after a clash followed them towards Lodi.

name: Neapolitan Cavlary position - Lodi
description: Sebottendorf had eight squadrons of Neapolitan cavalry at his disposal, Cavalry Regiments Re and Regina, and he positioned them around, just half hour form Lodi.

name: Lodi bridge
description: The iconic bridge over the Adda river where Napoleon gained the nickname 'Le Petit Caporal'.

name: Battlefield of Borghetto
description: On May 30th 1796, a French army led by General Napoleon Bonaparte forced a crossing of the Mincio River in the face of opposition from an Austrian army commanded by Feldzeugmeister Johann Peter Beaulieu.

name: Palazzo Guarienti
description: At the evening of the battle of Borghetto, Napoleon lodged at this building and was risked being captured by the Austrians who had suddenly arrived in the village.

name: Napoleon Monument
description: 4.5 m bronze Statue of Napoleon at Insurgents' Square, formerly Napoleon Square, inaugurated in 2011. Replica of the one erected in 1923.

name: Grave General Mazovskiy - Friedland
description: Commander of the Pavlovsky Grenadier Regiment. He was wounded in combat and taken to a house in the city. When French troops entered the city, he was killed.

name: House Gral Mazovskiy laid wounded - Friedland
description: After the Battle of Friedland, the wounded Russian Major General Nikolai Nikolaevich Mazovsky was taken to this house. When the city was occupied by French troops, the general was bayoneted to death in this very house.

name: Monument to the Victims of the War of 1812 - Berezina
description: Erected in 2002, In 2002 to honor of the fallen at the tragic events of November 1812 during Napoleon's army's crossing of the Berezina River. The approach to the monument is paved with uneven cobblestones, symbolizing the difficult journey each fallen soldier endured on their way to the Berezina.

name: Upper bridge over the Berezina
description: Site where the upper bridge, used by the infantry, was constructed. In 2002 a milestone was erected to replace the destoyed ones built in 1901 at the site discoverd by Ivan Khrisonfonovich Kolodeyev.

name: Artillery bridge over the Berezina
description: Site where the artillery bridge was constructed.(Photo Karavanni zyntek).

name: Monument Battle of Berezina
description: Obelisk erected in 1912 to honor fallen Russian soldiers.

name: French soldiers memorial - Berezina
description: Inaugurated in 1997, it is a tribute to the soldiers of Napoleon's 'Grande Armée' who perished during the catastrophic Battle of Berezina.

name: Memorial chapel - Berezina
description: This memorial is a chapel-monument located dedicated to the fallen at the Battle of Berezina in 1812, one of the most dramatic episodes of the Napoleonic Wars.

name: Milestone - Berezina
description: Memorial erected in 1904 near the turnoff to Studenka on the road from Veselovo to Borisov.

name: Batteries at Borizov
description: The Borisov Batteries (or Zemlyanye Batarei) are a historic site of earthwork fortifications located in Borisov on the banks of the Berezina River to protect the bridgr. Construction began in the spring of 1812 but the invasion of Napoleon's army interrupted the works.

name: History Museum of Borizov
description: The exposition consists of six halls, where the main part is occupied by the events of 1812. On this topic, the museum has a unique collection of artifacts, which is constantly being updated.

name: Mass grave of Russian soldiers - Friedland
description: The mass grave where the fallen at the Battle of Friedland in 1807 were buried.

name: Action of 22 May 1812
location: off the coast of Penmarch, France
description: A small French squadron comprising the French frigates Ariane and Andromaque, and the brig Mameluck returning from a commerce raiding campaign in the Atlantic, met the 74-gun HMS Northumberland while trying the slip to Lorient through the British blockade.

name: Battle of Lyngør
description: Fought on 6 July 1812 between the British and Dano-Norwegian navies as part of the Gunboat War.

name: Battle of Nargö
description: Fought on July 11th 1811 between the Russian cutter Opyt and the British frigate HMS Salsette took place near the northern coast of Estonia by the island of Naissaar (Nargö) in the Baltic Sea, and ended in a British victory.

name: Battlefield of Blaauwberg
description: Also known as the Battle of Cape Town, it was a successful British amphibious operation during the War of the Third Coalition which lasted from 8 to 18 January 1806 and resulted in the capture of the Dutch Cape Colony.

name: Marshal Bernadotte's Birthplace
description: Birthplace and museum of Jean-Baptiste Jules Bernadotte, who became King of Sweden and Norway under the name Charles XIV John.

name: Marshal Poniatowski's Birthplace
description: The palace was commissioned by Count Wirich Philipp von Daun, and construction started in 1713 under the direction of architect Johann Lucas von Hildebrandt. Prince Józef Poniatowski was born in the palace on May 7th 1763.

name: Marshal Kellerman's Birthplace
description: He was born on May 28th 1735 into a Saxon noble family established in Strasbourg.

name: Marshall Kellerman Statue
description: Bronze statue done in 1935 by Léon-Alexandre Blanchot.

name: Marshall Kellerman Tomb
description: The Kellermann family tomb at Père-Lachaise Cemetery at Avenue des Acacias, with mention to the vistories of Valmy and Marengo.

name: Apelstein Nr. 1 Victor - Battle of Wachau
description: Victor, Duke of Beluno II Corps 20,000 men Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 2 original remains - Battle of Wachau
description: Remains of the original milestone placed in a wrong location

name: Liebertwolkwitz Russian-Prussian Monument
description: From here, the 2nd Column under General Prince Eugene of Württemberg opened the Battle of Wachau around 8 a.m. with 10,000 men.

name: Apelstein Nr. 2 Prince Eugene - Battle of Wachau
description: Prince Eugene of Württemberg 10,000 men. Erected in 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 3 Augereau - Battle of Wachau
description: Augereau Duke of Castiglione IX Corps 10000 men. Erected in 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 4 Kleist - Battle of Wachau
description: General von Kleist, 10,000 men. Erected in 1861 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 5 Lauriston - Battle of Wachau
description: Count Lauriston V Corps 15,000 men. Erected in 1861 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Napoleon's command post - Battle of Leipzig
description: Count Lauriston V Corps 15,000 men. Erected in 1861 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 6 Gortschakow - Battle of Wachau
description: Prince Gortschakoff 9000 men. Erected in 1861 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 7 Mac Donald - Battle of Wachau
description: Mac Donald, Duke of Tarentum, XI Corps, 15,000 men. Erected in 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 8 Mac Donald - Battle of Wachau
description: General Klenau 9th Austrian Corps 24,000 men. Erected in 1861 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 9 Mortier - Battle of Wachau
description: Mortier, Duke of Treviso. 2 divisions of the Young Guard, 12,000 men. Erected in 1861 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 10 Pahlen - Battle of Wachau
description: Count Pahlen III. 3000 cavalry. Erected in 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 11 Poniatovsky - Battle of Wachau
description: Prince Poniatovsky VIII Corps 8000 men. Erected in 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 12 Bianchi - Battle of Wachau
description: General Bianchi Austrian Reserve. Erected in 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Battlefield of Grijó
description: Fought on May 10th and 11th 1809, iteended in victory for the Anglo-Portuguese Army commanded by Lieutenant-General Sir Arthur Wellesley (the future 1st Duke of Wellington) over the French army commanded by Marshal Nicolas Soult during the Second French invasion of Portugal.

name: Battlefield of Braga
description: The Battle of Braga or Battle of Póvoa de Lanhoso or Battle of Carvalho d'Este (March 20th 1809) saw an Imperial French corps led by Marshal Jean-de-Dieu Soult attack a Portuguese army commanded by Baron Eben

name: Battlefield of Évora
description: An Imperial French division under Louis Henri Loison attacked a combined Portuguese-Spanish force led by Francisco de Paula Leite de Sousa.

name: Apelstein Nr. 13 Oudinot - Battle of Wachau
description: Oudinot, Duke of Reggio, 3rd and 4th Divisions of the Young Guard, 12,000 men. Erected in 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 14 Saint Priest - Battle of Möckern
description: Gen Lieu St. Priest 12000 M. Russians. Erected in 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 15 Compans - Battle of Möckern
description: General Compans 1st Division. Erected in 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 16 Langeron - Battle of Möckern
description: General Count Langeron 18500 M. Russians. Erected in 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 17  Lagrange - Battle of Möckern
description: General Lagrange 2nd Division. Erected in 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 18 von Sacken - Battle of Möckern
description: Lieutenant General von Sacken 9000 Russians Original: Gen. von Sacken 9000 Cavalry. Erected in 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 19 Frederic - Battle of Möckern
description: General Frederic 3rd Division. Erected in 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 20 von York - Battle of Möckern
description: Lieutenant General von York 1st Prussian Army Corps 21,000 men.Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 21 - Battle of Möckern
description: General Dombrowski, General Souham, General Delmas of the III Corps, totaling 12,000 men.Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Memorial crossing Duero River
description: Memorial erected in memory of the battles fought on the banks of the river Douro in 1809.

name: Battlefield of Redinha
description: It was a rearguard action which took place on March 12th 1811, during Masséna's retreat from Portugal as part of the Peninsular War, by a French division under Marshal Ney against a considerably larger Anglo-Portuguese force under Wellington.

name: Memorial El Bodon
description: Memorial erected in 2011 to commemorate the battle on September 25th 1811, between the invading napoleonic army and the anglo-luso coalition.

name: Battlefield of El Bodon
description: It was a rearguard action fought on September 25th 1811 by elements of the Anglo-Portuguese army against waves of French cavalry, supported by a division of infantry of the French army.

name: Apelstein Nr. 22 Gyulai- Battle of Lindenau
description: General Count Gyulai. III. Austrian Corps. 17,000 men. Erected: 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 23 Bertrand- Battle of Lindenau
description: General Count Bertrand, IV Corps, 10,000 men. Erected: 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 24 Frederick VI - Battle of Lindenau
description: Prince of Hesse-Homburg, 1st Colonel of the 3rd Austrian Corps. Erected: 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Monument to Colonel George Lake
description: This monument marks either where he fell or where he was buried by his men after the battle. It was erected by his brother officers as testimony of high regard and esteem.

name: Apelstein Nr. 25 Marmont - Battle of Möckern
description: Marmont Hereditary Duke of Ragusa IV Corps 18000 M. Erected: 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 26 Liechtenstein - Battle of Lindenau
description: Prince M. Liechtenstein I. Austrian Light Division 5500 M. Erected: 1862 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 27 Mac Donald - Battle of Leipzig
description: Mac Donald XI Corps, 12,000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 28 Bennigsen - Battle of Leipzig
description: III. Column General Count Bennigsen 65,000 M. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 29 Marmont - Battle of Schönefeld
description: Marshal Marmont VI Corps 15000 M. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Church of Saint Rocco - Lodi
description: Place where heavy fight between the Austrians and French took place. The image shows as it would be in 1796 with the 'Porta d'Adda' built in 1787.

name: Church of Saint Francis - Lodi
description: From the bell tower of the Church of San Francesco, Napoleon observed and directed the fighting.

name: Scene from the Battle of Lodi
description: Place for the scene of the iconic painting by Felician von Myrbach 'Bonaparte Aiming the Cannon at Lodi'. It depicts Napoleon supervising the position of the French artillery. This action earned him the affectionate nickname 'The Little Corporal' (le petit caporal) from his soldiers.

name: Torre del Puerco
description: Constructed in the XVI century to defend the Spanish coasts from the pirates, it was the testimonial of the Battle of Chiclana between the Anglo-Spanish and French troops and is in the background of the painting by the artist Louis-François Lejeune: Battle of Chiclana.

name: Apelstein Nr. 30 Langeron -Battle of Schoeneufeld
description: Gen. Count LANGERON 30000M. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 31 Lauriston  - Battle of Leipzig
description: Victor Lauriston II and V Corps, 30,000 men. Erected: 1861 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 32 Barclay de Tolly - Battle of Leipzig
description: II. Column General Barclai de Tolly, 50,000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Monarchenhügel (Monarch's Hill)  - Battle of Leipzig
description: The monument commemorates the Battle of Leipzig , where the three allied monarchs observed the course of the battle from here and learned of Napoleon's retreat.

name: Tomb of the Maréchal Guillaume Brune
description: Here rests the Marshal Guillaume Brune, victim of a popular uprising in Avignon in August 1815, as he was returning to France to serve the Emperor in the Hundred Days campaign.

name: Place death of the Maréchal Brune
description: Place where Marshall Brune was killed in August 1815, victim of a popular uprising in Avignon, as he was returning to France to serve the Emperor in the Hundred Days campaign.

name: Birthplace of the Maréchal Brune
description: Maréchal Brune was born on March 13th 1764 at this place.

name: Statue of the Maréchal Brune
description: Statue of Maréchal Brune at his natal town of Brive la Gaillarde. Erected on September 23, 1841, and officially inaugurated on October 3, 1841.

name: Monumento Napoleonico Rivoli Veronesi
description: This monument is dedicated to the memory of the heroes of the Napoleonic era.

name: Statue General Cambronne
description: Bronze statue by Jean-Baptiste Joseph Debay fils erected in 1847.

name: Battlefield of Maloyaroslavets
description: Took place on October 24th 1812. Russian and French armies were evenly matched in the battle and suffered similar casualties, with a marginal edge going to the Franco-Italian army.

name: Battlefield First battle of Polotsk
description: Took place on October 18th 1812. Russian troops under the general command of Bennigsen, acting on instructions from Kutuzov and being part of his army, launched a surprise attack and defeated French troops under Murat's command.

name: Battlefield of Wolkowisk (Volkovysk)
description: Took place from November 14th to the 16th 1812. Austrian, Saxon and French soldiers under Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg defeated the Russian army under Fabian Gottlieb von der Osten-Sacken

name: History Museum Bagration
description: The History Museum Bagration in Volkovysk has a collection from the 1812 Russian campaign.

name: Battlefield of Borizov
description: Took place on November 21st 1812, between parts of the French army and parts of the Russian armies. The Russian vanguard under Charles de Lambert eliminated the Franco-Polish force under Dombrowsky.

name: Battlefield of Swolna
description: Took place on August 11th 1812 near the village of Svolna where Oudinot's French ran at the river Svolna into the Russians under d'Auvray.

name: Battlefield of Mir
description: Took place on 9 and 10 July 1812 when three Polish Lancers divisions battled against Russian cavalry, ending in the first major Russian victory in the war.

name: Apelstein Nr. 33 Poniatowski | Augureau | Oudinot - Battle of Leipzig
description: PONIATOWSKI AUGEREAU VIII and IX Corps.OUDINOT III and IV Divisions of the Young Guard, 30,000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Austrian monument 1st Corp - Battle of Leipzig
description: One of four monuments dating from 1913, identical except for their inscriptions, commemorating the successful participation of Austrian troops in the Battle of Leipzig.

name: Austrian monuments 2nd Light Division - Battle of Leipzig
description: One of four monuments dating from 1913, identical except for their inscriptions, commemorating the successful participation of Austrian troops in the Battle of Leipzig.

name: Austrian monuments 3rd Corps - Battle of Leipzig
description: One of four monuments dating from 1913, identical except for their inscriptions, commemorating the successful participation of Austrian troops in the Battle of Leipzig.

name: Austrian monuments 4th Corps - Battle of Leipzig
description: One of four monuments dating from 1913, identical except for their inscriptions, commemorating the successful participation of Austrian troops in the Battle of Leipzig.

name: Apelstein Nr. 34 Friedrich VI | Nostitz - Battle of Leipzig
description: 1st Colonel, Hereditary Prince of Hesse-Homburg, later General von Nostitz, 50,000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 35 Mortier - Battle of Leipzig
description: Marshal Mortier, 1st and 2nd Divisions of the Young Guard, 10,000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 36 Blücher - Battle of Leipzig
description: V. Colonne Gener. BLUECHER Schles. Army 25000 M. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 37 Dombrowski - Battle of Leipzig
description: General Dombrowski 5000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 38 Bernadotte - Battle of Leipzig
description: Bernadotte CARL JOHANN Crown Prince of Sweden 50000 M. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel (photo by: Medien-GbR.de).

name: Apelstein Nr. 39 Reynier - Battle of Leipzig
description: GENERAL REYNIER VII Corps 10,000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 40 Bogue - Battle of Leipzig
description: Captain Bogue English rocket battery. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 41 Ney - Battle of Leipzig
description: Ney Prince of Moscow, left French wing. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 42 Bülow - Battle of Leipzig
description: General BUELOW Count von Dennewitz III Prussian Corps 24,000 men. Erected: 1863 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 43 Friccius - Battle of Leipzig
description: Major Carl Friccius 3rd Königsberg Battalion of the 3rd East Prussian Landwehr Infantry Regiment. Erected: 1864 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 44 Friccius - Battle of Möckern
description: Landwehr from October 15th to 16th, 1813 before the Battle of Möckern. Erected: 1864 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Apelstein Nr. 45 Monarchs - Battle of Leipzig
description: The monarchs receiving the council delegation, October 19, 1813. Erected: 1864 by Dr. Theodor Apel.

name: Siege of Lugo
description: General Nicolas Mahy intended to caputre Lugo defended by French General Fournier who was reinforced on May 22nd 1809 by Soult with 19.000 men. Mahy retreated to Villalba declining to confront a force triple as his 6.000 men.

name: Pałac von Buddenbrock Pielaszkowice (ruins)
description: At this place Napoleon signed the Tripartite Armistice on June 4, 1813.

name: Battle of Talavera - Wellington Command Post
description: Arthur Wellesley's Command Post at 'Cerro Medellín' during the Battle of Talavera.

name: Battle of Talavera - Monument
description: Located south of Cerro de Medellín, one of the key locations of the battle. Its height is 27 meters and it was built between 1989 and 1990 and inaugurated on October 2, 1990.

name: Equestrian statue of Napoleon
description: The equestrian statue depicts Napoleon on horseback and was erected in 1865. It was sculpted in bronze by Gabriel-Vital Dubray.

name: Joseph Bonaparte's house 1815-1816
description: Joseph Bonaparte, king of Naples & Spain lived here, settled here after Napoloen's fall in 1815 and before returning to Europe in 1816.
